title: Quick & Dirty DNA Extraction
author: Sam Mantel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baf7ibrn

steps.instruction: Pre-heat Quick & Dirty extraction buffer with detergent in a 60 °C incubator.
Place one steel ball bearing into each sample tube.
When tissue is ground to a fine powder centrifuge briefly to remove powder from the inside of caps.
Add roughly 200 mL nanopure H 2 O to a 1 L glass bottle with a stir bar and stir.

Snap bottom back on to plate and shake in tissue homogenizer for 00:00:30 on highest seed. Note: Shake plate without lid so that casing of the homogenizer holds caps in place. Distribute samples evenly across plate so that samples are ground evenly.
Add 350 µL pre-heated Q&D extraction buffer with detergent to each sample.
Incubate samples at 60 °C for 00:15:00 to 01:00:00 . Note: To prevent caps from popping off while incubating, place inverted plate bottom over caps and hold down with a weight.
Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 22 °C, 20 minutes to pellet tissue.
Return to 1 L bottle and autoclave for 00:25:00 .
Gently add 100 mL 5% sarkosyl solution to Q&D buffer. Note: Sarkosyl solution is a a detergent and will foam if poured to quickly.
Near the end of the spin, transfer 200 µL per sample ice cold isopropanol to a new plate.
Transfer 80 mL 1 M Tris base to bottle.
Transfer 56 mL 0.5 M EDTA to bottle.
Transfer 46.7 g NaCl to bottle.
Transfer 3 g sodium metabisulfite to bottle.
Stir until dissolved.
Transfer solution to graduated cylinder and add nanopure H 2 O to bring volume to 400 mL .
Allow solution to cool to room temperature.
Swirl solution gently to mix. Note: Once detergent has been added, do not autoclave, detergent will degrade at high heat.
Remove plate from -80 and quickly remove bottom from plate and float in liquid nitrogen to keep tissue frozen.
Open tubes and discard caps.
Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.
Add 150 µL 1 M ammonium acetate to each sample.
Invert samples gently every 00:05:00 . Note: Caps will likely be loose, close tubes completely before inverting.
Place isopropanol, 70% ethanol, and 95% ethanol in the freezer to chill.
Transfer 300 µL , or as much as you can, of supernatant from each sample to isopropanol plate.
Recap tubes and invert several times to mix.
Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 to 00:15:00
Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 20 minutes to pellet DNA.
Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.
Add 500 µL ice cold 70% ethanol to each sample.
Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.
Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes to wash pellet.
Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.
Add 500 µL ice cold 95% ethanol to each sample.
Cover with clean caps and invert gently to mix.
Centrifuge 3700 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes to wash pellet.
Slowly pour off supernatant without disturbing pelleted DNA.
Place the flat on a paper towel and cover the open tubes with a KimWipe.
Allow pellets to dry overnight.

Once pellet is dry, and no residual liquid remains in the tubes, re-suspend pellets with 20 µL - 100 µL of TE buffer or nanopure H 2 O depending on pellet size, desired concentration, and future uses.
